title: St. Andrew's Basilica, Arthunkal
tagline: St. Andrew's Basilica, Arthunkal — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: St. Andrew's Basilica, Arthunkal is a much-loved spiritual town in Kerala, known for temples and pilgrimage.
overview.description: St. Andrew's Basilica, Arthunkal is a basilica located in the village of Arthunkal in Alappuzha, Kerala, India. It was constructed by Portuguese missionaries in the 16th century. The grand annual feast of St.

topPlaces.name: Arthunkal
topPlaces.description: Arthunkal is a coastal town and a major pilgrim centre in the south Indian state of Kerala. It is 40 kilometre south of Cochin city and 21 kilometre north of Alleppey town.

topPlaces.name: Cherthala North
topPlaces.description: Cherthala North is a village in Alappuzha district in the Indian state of Kerala.

topPlaces.name: Cherthala South
topPlaces.description: Cherthala South is a village in Alappuzha district in the Indian state of Kerala.

topPlaces.name: Kuttikattu Sree Bhadra Kali Devi Temple
topPlaces.description: Kuttikattu Sree Bhadra Kali Devi Temple is one of the Bhadrakali temples in Cherthala, Alappuzha district, Kerala. It is about 1 kilometer west of the Arthungal bypass of NH 47 at Cherthala.

image.alt: Kuttikattu Sree Bhadra Kali Devi Temple

topPlaces.name: Kanichukulangara Devi Temple
topPlaces.description: Kanichukulangara Temple (referred to as Kanichukulangara Bhagwathy Temple) is a famous temple of the Hindu mother goddess Bhagawathi. The temple is located near Cherthala in Alappuzha district of Kerala in India.

topPlaces.name: Kadakkarappally
topPlaces.description: Kadakkarappally is a village in Alappuzha district in the Indian state of Kerala. It is a panchayat which comes under the Cherthala Assembly constituency.

topPlaces.name: Velorvattom Sri Mahadeva Temple
topPlaces.description: Velorvattom Sri Mahadeva Temple is a Hindu temple located in Cherthala in Alleppey district in the Indian state of Kerala. Believed to be constructed in the 7th century by Azhvanchery Thamprakkal.

topPlaces.name: Cherthala
topPlaces.description: Cherthala is a suburb and satellite town of Kochi City located in the Alappuzha district of Kerala, India, along National Highway 66. As per the 2011 Indian census, Cherthala has a population of 45,821 people, and a population density of 2,727/km2 (7,060/sq mi).

faq.q: What is the best time to visit St. Andrew's Basilica, Arthunkal?
faq.a: The best time to visit St. Andrew's Basilica, Arthunkal is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach St. Andrew's Basilica, Arthunkal?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Cochin International Airport, Kochi (56 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (34 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for St. Andrew's Basilica, Arthunkal?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of St. Andrew's Basilica, Arthunkal at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in St. Andrew's Basilica, Arthunkal?
faq.a: Stays in St. Andrew's Basilica, Arthunkal start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in St. Andrew's Basilica, Arthunkal?
faq.a: The top attractions are Arthunkal, Cherthala North, Cherthala South, Kuttikattu Sree Bhadra Kali Devi Temple, Kanichukulangara Devi Temple.
